steps.title: Calculate wax consumed
steps.content:
The candle starts with 17 ounces and now has 6 ounces remaining. The amount of wax that has burned is:
$$17 - 6 = 11 \text{ ounces}$$

steps.title: Convert burn rate to time
steps.content:
The wax decreases at a rate of 1 ounce per 4 hours. To find how long 11 ounces takes to burn, multiply the amount by the time per ounce:
$$11 \text{ ounces} \times \frac{4 \text{ hours}}{1 \text{ ounce}} = 44 \text{ hours}$$
That confirms D is correct.
